I'll start by reading the file to understand the current code.

Now let me analyze each fix:

### Fix 1 Analysis (Line 269 - Button variant)
Line 269: `variant={ButtonVariant.link}` — The type of `Button.variant` changed to add `'stateful'` to the union. The current value `ButtonVariant.link` is `'link'`, which is still valid in the new type. **No code change is needed** — the value `link` is still in the new union type.

### Fix 2 Analysis (Line 166 - Card isFlat)
Line 166: `<Card className={classNames(className)} isFlat={true}>` — The `isFlat` prop has been removed in PF v6. According to the PF v6 migration guide, `isFlat` was removed because cards are now flat by default in v6. The fix is to simply remove `isFlat={true}` since flat is the default appearance.

Let me apply the change:
Now let me verify the final file looks correct:
The edit is correct. Let me also verify the Button variant area is unchanged (Fix 1 needs no change):

</GridItem>

              <Button
                variant={ButtonVariant.link}
                icon={<ArrowRightIcon />}
                iconPosition="end"
                className="pf-v6-u-font-size-lg odf-cluster-card__storage-link"

content.text:
Good — the Button variant line is untouched and correct (`link` is still a valid value in the new union type).

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Status | Details |
|-----|--------|---------|
| **Fix 1** (Line 269 — `Button.variant` type changed) | **Already valid — no change needed** | The value `ButtonVariant.link` (`'link'`) remains valid in the new union type. The type change only *added* `'stateful'` as a new option; no existing values were removed. |
| **Fix 2** (Line 166 — `Card.isFlat` removed prop) | **Applied** | Removed `isFlat={true}` from the `<Card>` element. In PatternFly v6, cards are flat by default, so `isFlat` was removed as unnecessary. The visual appearance is preserved since flat is now the default. |

I'll analyze both fixes carefully before making changes.

**Fix 1 & Fix 2 Analysis:**
Both fixes relate to `ChartDonutProps` and `ChartLabelProps` interfaces being "removed" from `@patternfly/react-charts`. However, looking at the fix strategies:
- Both have `From: ChartDonutProps` → `To: ChartDonutProps` and `From: ChartLabelProps` → `To: ChartLabelProps` (same names)
- All member mappings are identity mappings (prop → same prop)
- The components `ChartDonut`, `ChartLabel` are imported and used, not their Props interfaces

This means the interfaces were likely just re-exported differently or restructured internally, but the component API remains the same. The import is of the **components** (`ChartDonut`, `ChartLabel`), not the Props interfaces. Since the components still exist with the same props, and this file doesn't reference `ChartDonutProps` or `ChartLabelProps` directly, no code changes are needed.

All props used on `<ChartDonut>` (lines 215-262) map 1:1 to the new interface. The import at line 47 is of the component, not the Props type. No change needed.

**Fix 2 (ChartLabelProps removed):** Same situation - the file imports `ChartLabel` (the component), not `ChartLabelProps` (the interface). The component is used at lines 228-234 and 237-241. All props used (`className`, `style`, `dy`) map 1:1. No change needed.

- Fix 1 (Button.variant): Line 269 shows `variant={ButtonVariant.link}` - confirmed still valid ✓

### Fix 2
Line: 268
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: GridItem

  After:  <DescriptionListTerm variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

  After:  <DescriptionListTerm icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</DescriptionListTerm>
